title: Income Approach: If a commercial property generates 600000 in gross revenue and...
summary: Calculate or interpret NOI in real estate investment analysis.
prompt: If a commercial property generates $600,000 in gross revenue and has operating expenses of $350,000, what is the net operating income (NOI)?
solution: The Net Operating Income (NOI) is calculated as follows: NOI = Gross Revenue - Operating Expenses. Here, NOI = $600,000 - $350,000 = $250,000. Understanding NOI is crucial for assessing property performance and potential profitability, as it serves as a key measure for real estate investors when analyzing cash flow and investment opportunities. This financial metric is fundamental in the valuation and investment decision-making processes.
